title: 1981-04-16_Pat_143.txt
key_claims: Christianity, like Buddhism, addresses the problem of suffering, but solves it through a unique miracle: it alchemically converts suffering into its 180-degree opposite—ecstasy. This was the authentic payoff of Dick's 1974 experience.
The Christian via (path) demands the voluntary taking on of suffering (*Agape*) in imitation of Christ. Only by going to the deepest layer of suffering can it undergo *enantiodromia* into joy.
The true Christian exists in the "tiny interval" between Christ's departure and His imminent return. The soul acts as the Bride, joyfully preparing for the wedding (hierogamy) with the Bridegroom.
Birth in the Spirit provides direct knowledge of Christ and initiates this spiritual union before death.
recurring_concepts: **Enantiodromia**: The psychological mechanism by which extreme suffering and grief necessarily reverse into their absolute opposite polarity (ecstatic joy).
reading_excerpt: Christianity, like Buddhism, addresses the problem of suffering, but solves it through a unique miracle: it alchemically converts suffering into its 180-degree opposite—ecstasy. This was the authentic payoff of Dick's 1974 experience.
